content.summary: Converts SOP geometry attributes (position, normals, UVs, custom attributes) into TOP textures so they can be processed on the GPU. This is a foundational technique for GPU-based particle systems, instancing, and any workflow that needs to move per-point data from the CPU geometry pipeline into the texture/compute pipeline.

codeSnippets.label: Read back pixel data from SOP-to-TOP conversion
codeSnippets.code:
# Read position data from a soptoTOP
sop_tex = op('soptoTOP1')
width = sop_tex.width
height = sop_tex.height

# Access pixel values (each pixel = one point's data)
for i in range(width):
    r, g, b, a = sop_tex.sample(x=i, y=0)
    print(f'Point {i}: pos=({r}, {g}, {b})')
codeSnippets.description: Demonstrates reading position data back from the texture. In practice you would keep data on the GPU, but this is useful for debugging.

codeSnippets.label: Configure SOP to TOP for position output
codeSnippets.code:
# Configure a SOP to TOP to output point positions as 32-bit float
sop_to_top = op('soptoTOP1')
sop_to_top.par.sop = op('sphere1')
sop_to_top.par.outputattribute = 'P'
sop_to_top.par.format = 'Use Input'
codeSnippets.description: Sets up the SOP to TOP operator to extract position attribute from the source SOP.

tips: Use 32-bit float format on the SOP to TOP to preserve precision for position data.
Sort the SOP points before conversion to ensure consistent pixel-to-point mapping across frames.
Chain multiple SOP to TOPs for different attributes (P, N, Cd) and combine in a GLSL TOP.
